claim: The Model Context Protocol is an open standard for connecting LLMs to external data sources and systems, designed to work with any LLM, not just Anthropic models
actionable: Build MCP servers using the TypeScript or Python SDK that expose tools and data sources; connect them to any LLM via MCP hosts like Claude desktop.

claim: MCP servers are scripts that live on your local device and expose tool definitions and schemas so LLMs know exactly what parameters they accept
actionable: Create MCP server implementations that define tool schemas with input/output types; publish them so hosts can discover and use them automatically.

claim: The real power of LLMs comes from what you put in the context window; getting the right data at the right time is more important than model upgrades
actionable: Invest in context engineering and data retrieval systems before chasing model upgrades; prioritize feeding quality, relevant context to your LLM.

claim: MCP servers can call external APIs from cloud databases like Chroma or Pinecone, allowing local hosts to wire up to cloud infrastructure
actionable: Build MCP servers that make external API calls to cloud RAG or vector databases; this keeps the host local while accessing remote data.

claim: You configure Claude desktop to use MCP servers by creating a config.json file in the application support directory with server definitions
actionable: Create .claude/settings.json or claude_desktop_config.json with an 'mcpServers' object pointing to your local or remote server implementations.
